decision support · PR #3056 · orchestkit

Reachability costs tokens
how much of the fix should survive?

Eleven skills were reachable by nobody. Making them slash-invocable fixed that, but every slash command is enumerated in the system prompt on every session. Toggle skills below to see the real cost.

Correction. I said repeatedly that this change was free because disable-model-invocation: true keeps descriptions out of context. That part is true, but incomplete: the slash command itself still costs roughly 50 to 100 tokens each. The repo enforces this with a hard cap that has been raised deliberately, one or two at a time, each with an issue number.

Pick a set

Evidence per skill: whether its own SKILL.md tells a user to type /ork:<name> (a command that did not exist), and whether it declares argument-hint, which only makes sense for a slash command.

45
user-invocable skills
34 → 45
cap change needed
+550-1100
tokens per session, forever
FAIL
token-overhead gate
bar = user-invocable count| line = current cap (34)
SkillOwn docs say /ork:argument-hintCase
-

Two gates pulling opposite ways

This is why the fix was not obviously right. One gate wants skills reachable; another caps how many may be reachable.

flowchart LR
  U["unreachable<br/>no slash · no model · no agent"] --> A["user-invocable: true"]
  U --> B["agent skills: array"]
  A --> I{"island gate<br/>automated channel?"}
  A --> C{"token cap 34<br/>~50-100 tok each"}
  B --> I
  I -->|no| X["FAIL · allowlist with a reason"]
  C -->|over| Y["FAIL · raise cap, costs every session"]
  I -->|yes| P["pass"]
  C -->|under| P

The cap has never jumped like this

Each raise carries an issue number in the source comment. Keeping all 11 would be the largest single increase by a wide margin.

23
25
26
27
28
29
33
34
45
Session overhead is already ~7005 tokens against a 5500 budget, measured before this change. The cap is not the only thing under pressure.

What I would do

Keep the four with broken documented contracts. Those skills tell users to type a command that did not exist: feedback says it 14 times, skill-evolution 12. That is a real bug and worth ~200-400 tokens. The other seven were only ever reachable in theory, nobody has missed them, and the session budget is already over. Revert those.
